name: Mid-level designer with URL — tests multi-source synthesis

input.userMessage: TeamPulse is a team collaboration platform for distributed teams. Think Notion meets Slack. Main features: activity feeds, team profiles, internal wiki. Primary users are ICs who use it daily, team leads who stay informed. Planning notification center and team settings next.

expectedBehaviors: Fetches notion.so and extracts navigation, layout patterns, terminology
Identifies all 8 existing Figma frames by name and type
Reconciles user input with Figma analysis (both describe feeds, profiles, wiki)
Populates screenInventory.designed with the 8 existing frames
Populates screenInventory.planned with notification center and team settings
Captures both user types: ICs (primary) and team leads (secondary)
Lists Notion and Slack as competitors
PRESENT summary mentions the existing frames and planned screens
antiPatterns: Does not fetch notion.so when explicitly provided
Ignores Figma file analysis and relies only on user input
PRESENT summary does not mention the existing 8 frames
Contradicts user's input about features or user types
Overwrites user-stated terminology with inferred alternatives

evaluationCriteria.urlExtraction: Did it actually fetch and extract patterns from notion.so?
evaluationCriteria.sourceSynthesis: Does the output reconcile user input + URL data + Figma analysis?
evaluationCriteria.screenInventory: Are designed, planned, and notStarted screens accurately categorized?
evaluationCriteria.competitorAwareness: Does it identify relevant competitors from context?
